grammar.name: ✅ Finite Indicative Active Past Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the past tense, e.g., 'I was...', 'I chose...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I was disciplined to build a stable foundation for my life
I chose wisdom to navigate the complexities of my career
I found my strength in maintaining the order of my daily routine

grammar.name: ⭐ Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am ready to lead my life into a structured and clear future
I trust that my discipline will bring order to the chaos of my daily work
I am worthy of the authority that guides my path forward

grammar.name: ⏭️ Finite Indicative Active Future Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the future tense with 'will': 'I will...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I will establish a system that brings clarity to chaos
I will govern my life as a kingdom with unwavering confidence
I will create order through disciplined routine and hard work

examples: I have been building my resilience.
I have been growing every day.
grammar.name: 🔄 Finite Indicative Active Present Perfect Progressive First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the perfect progressive: 'I have been [verb]ing...'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I have been building my resilience
I have been choosing kindness
I have been growing every day

examples: I will have overcome this challenge.
I will have become the person I am meant to be.
I will have created a life I love.
grammar.name: 🎯 Finite Indicative Active Future Perfect Perfective First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the future perfect tense: 'I will have [past participle]...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I will have mastered the art of strategic planning that guides my life
I will have become the leader who brings order to the chaos of daily life
I will have created a system of discipline that secures my future and honors my authority

grammar.name: 🤝 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Plural
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'we' (first person plural).

affirmations.text: We are the leaders who bring order to the chaos of our daily lives
We stand as the guardians who ensure stability in our personal and professional paths
We are the architects who build systems that guide us toward clarity and long-term success

examples: They believe in their own power.
grammar.name: 👤 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple Third
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is third-person: 'he', 'she', 'it', or 'they'.

affirmations.text: I am the one who sets the course for our journey
I trust that I will bring order to the chaos of our lives
I am the architect of stability within our personal and professional worlds

examples: I can handle whatever comes my way.
I could become who I dream of being.
grammar.name: 💪 Finite Potential Active Present Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Express capability or possibility using 'can' or 'could'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I can bring order and stability to my daily life
I could establish a routine that guides my actions
I am ready to lead with wisdom and discipline

examples: May I find clarity in this moment.
If only I could feel at peace.
Would that I might know my own worth.
grammar.name: 🙏 Finite Optative Active First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Express a wish or hope, e.g., 'May I find...', 'If only I could...'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: May I find clarity in this chaotic moment?
If only I could bring order to my daily life?
Would that I might establish a system for my own success?

grammar.name: ❗ Finite Imperative Active Second Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Give a direct command (imperative mood), e.g., 'Be bold!'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Subject/address is 'you' (second person).

affirmations.text: Be bold! You are the king who rules the chaos of your life
Trust yourself! You hold the power to build a kingdom of your own
Embrace your greatness! Your authority creates a stable order for all who seek it

examples: Am I not capable of this?
Have I not already proven my resilience?
Can I not rise to this challenge?
grammar.name: ❓ Finite Interrogative Active Present First Singular Negative
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- CRITICAL: The sentence MUST be phrased as a QUESTION ending with '?', e.g., 'Am I not...?', 'Have I not...?', 'Can I not...?'. Do NOT write a declarative statement.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Include negation using 'not', 'never', 'no longer', or 'without'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: Am I not capable of leading this chaotic situation with unwavering confidence?
Have I not already proven my ability to bring clarity to the chaos of our current plans?
Can I not rise to this challenge without losing control over my own life?

grammar.name: ♾️ Infinitive Active Simple
grammar.description:
- Start with the base infinitive: 'To [verb]...' — no subject or conjugation.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.

affirmations.text: To establish a clear path for your future
To lead with wisdom and structure
To build a kingdom of your own

grammar.name: 💡 Gerund Active
grammar.description:
- Begin with a gerund (-ing form as a noun), e.g., 'Being brave is...'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.

affirmations.text: Being disciplined is my strength
Being organized is my strength
Being strategic is my strength

examples: I am supported by those around me.
grammar.name: 🌊 Finite Indicative Passive Present Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- CRITICAL: Use PASSIVE voice — the subject receives the action. Pattern: 'I am/was/have been [past participle]', e.g., 'I am loved', 'I am guided', 'I am supported by...'. Do NOT write active sentences like 'I trust' or 'I choose'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am guided by the wisdom of the crown
I am supported by the authority of the throne
I am ruled by the necessity of order

examples: I consistently show up for myself.
grammar.name: 🔁 Finite Indicative Active Present Habitual First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express a recurring action using 'always', 'regularly', 'consistently', or 'every day'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I always maintain strict discipline to ensure my life remains a kingdom of order
I consistently apply logic and strategy to navigate the complexities of daily challenges
I regularly uphold the principles of authority and stability in every aspect of my existence

examples: I am not defined by my past.
I am not afraid of change.
I do not limit my own potential.
grammar.name: 🫷 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Singular Negative
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Include negation using 'not', 'never', 'no longer', or 'without'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am not defined by my past
I am not afraid of change
I do not limit my own potential
